method_atom: Exploiting conformal (scale- and rotation-invariant) symmetry at a critical point to derive exact closed-form formulas for macroscopic spanning/connectivity observables, working within a two-dimensional field theory characterized by a central charge and an algebra of primary local operators. Key operator identities — including identifying the derivative of one primary operator with another — are used to integrate several results into a unified derivation and to expose an additional symmetry present when the central charge vanishes. Predictions are cross-checked against high-precision numerical simulation.
object_atom: Two-dimensional percolation at its critical point: the probability that random clusters span a rectangular region horizontally, the probability they span both horizontally and vertically, and the expected number of clusters crossing the region horizontally.
brief_reason: The conformal-symmetry machinery, primary-operator algebra, and operator identities that yield exact critical observables carry across fields, while the percolation clusters and their horizontal/vertical crossing probabilities are the domain-specific object.
